rationale.R1.2: The methods section makes interleaved technical claims (meta-gradient update, residual-adaptive sampling, gradient-balancing) whose internal consistency and logical coherence are a plausible central responsibility under any reading of 'help'.
rationale.R1.3: Five distinct citation-anchored claims are made (Krishnapriyan 2021, Wang/Yu/Perdikaris 2022, Rahaman 2019, Tancik 2020, Wang et al. 2021, Wu 2023); verifying that each cited result actually supports the attributed claim is central if the task is content review.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is the most common surface interpretation of 'help me with this section' — sentence clarity, transitions between the background and method paragraphs, and consistency of notation (e.g., B used both as a matrix and implicitly as a sampling variable) are directly improvable.
rationale.R1.7: The related-work paragraph contains six inline citations; accuracy of author lists, years, and attributed findings is a distinct load-bearing concern if the task is scholarship review.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want primarily writing-quality improvements (prose clarity, style, notation consistency) rather than a technical or scientific content review (logical soundness, citation accuracy, evidence-claim alignment)?
